lines.english: He came into the world to obtain the four great blessings.
He came to dwell in the home of the Shiva and Shakti, energy and matter.
The child dies in his childish games.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕੁ ਵਿਸਾਰੇ ਤਾ ਪਿੜ ਹਾਰੇ ਅੰਧੁਲੈ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: ek visaare taa pirr haare andhulai naam visaaraa he |6|
lines.english: But he forgot the One Lord, and he has lost the game. The blind person forgets the Naam, the Name of the Lord. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਰਿ ਜੋਬਨਿ ਮਰਿ ਜਾਹਿ ਕਿ ਕੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: bhar joban mar jaeh ki keejai |
lines.english: What can they do, if he dies in his youth?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਕਾ ਸਾ ਸੋ ਤਿਨ ਹੀ ਲੀਆ ਭੂਲਾ ਰੋਵਣਹਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: jis kaa saa so tin hee leea bhoolaa rovanahaaraa he |7|
lines.english: The Lord who owns him has taken him back. Those who weep and mourn are mistaken. ||7||
They cry and mourn, saying that he was such a playful child.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਕਾਰਣਿ ਰੋਇ ਵਿਗੂਚਹਿ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਜੀਵਣੁ ਸੰਸਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: maaeaa kaaran roe vigoocheh dhrig jeevan sansaaraa he |8|
lines.english: They cry for the sake of Maya, and are ruined; their lives in this world are cursed. ||8||
They cry out, "His is mine, he is mine!"
Without the Name, they lose their wealth, and then leave.
Their black hair eventually turns grey.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਅੰਧੁਲਾ ਬਿਨਸਿ ਬਿਨਾਸੈ ਮੂਠੇ ਰੋਇ ਪੂਕਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: duramat andhulaa binas binaasai mootthe roe pookaaraa he |9|
lines.english: They are evil-minded and blind - they are totally ruined; they are plundered, and cry out in pain. ||9||
One who understands himself, does not cry.
When he meets the True Guru, then he understands.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਬਜਰ ਕਪਾਟ ਨ ਖੂਲਹਿ ਸਬਦਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਨਿਸਤਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: bin gur bajar kapaatt na khooleh sabad milai nisataaraa he |10|
lines.english: Without the Guru, the heavy, hard doors are not opened. Obtaining the Word of the Shabad, one is emancipated. ||10||
But he does not meditate on the Lord, His only friend, even at the end.
The body grows old, and is beaten out of shape.
Forgetting the Naam, the false ones depart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿ ਚਲੈ ਮੁਹਿ ਕਾਲੈ ਦਰਗਹ ਝੂਠੁ ਖੁਆਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: naam visaar chalai muhi kaalai daragah jhootth khuaaraa he |11|
lines.english: Forgetting the Naam, the Name of the Lord, he departs with his face blackened. The false are humiliated in the Court of the Lord. ||11||
Coming and going, dust falls on their heads.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹੁਰੜੈ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸੁ ਨ ਪਾਏ ਪੇਈਅੜੈ ਸਿਰਿ ਮਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: saahurarrai ghar vaas na paae peeearrai sir maaraa he |12|
lines.english: The soul-bride finds no home in her in-laws' home, the world hereafter; she suffers in agony in this world of her parents' home. ||12||
but without loving devotional worship of the Lord, she dies uselessly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰ ਅਪਸਰ ਕੀ ਸਾਰ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਜਮੁ ਮਾਰੇ ਕਿਆ ਚਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: sar apasar kee saar na jaanai jam maare kiaa chaaraa he |13|
lines.english: One who does not distinguish between good and evil, is beaten by the Messenger of Death; how can anyone escape this? ||13||
She eats, dresses and plays joyfully,
One who realizes what he has to possess, and what he has to abandon,

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਸਬਦਿ ਘਰੁ ਜਾਣੈ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai sang sabad ghar jaanai |
lines.english: associating with the Guru, comes to know the Word of the Shabad, within the home of his own self.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਸ ਹੀ ਮੰਦਾ ਆਖਿ ਨ ਚਲੈ ਸਚਿ ਖਰਾ ਸਚਿਆਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: kis hee mandaa aakh na chalai sach kharaa sachiaaraa he |14|
lines.english: Do not call anyone else bad; follow this way of life. Those who are true are judged to be genuine by the True Lord. ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚ ਬਿਨਾ ਦਰਿ ਸਿਝੈ ਨ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: saach binaa dar sijhai na koee |
lines.english: Without Truth, no one succeeds in the Court of the Lord.
Through the True Shabad, one is robed in honor.
comes to know the lifestyle of the ages.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਬਖਸਿ ਲਏ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਹਉਮੈ ਗਰਬੁ ਨਿਵਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: aape bakhas le tis bhaavai haumai garab nivaaraa he |15|
lines.english: He forgives those with whom He is pleased; they silence their egotism and pride. ||15||
One who realizes the Hukam of God's Command, by the Grace of the Guru,

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਹੁ ਤਰੁ ਤਾਰੀ ਸਚੁ ਤਾਰੇ ਤਾਰਣਹਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੬॥੧॥੭॥
lines.translit: naanak naam japahu tar taaree sach taare taaranahaaraa he |16|1|7|
lines.english: O Nanak, chant the Naam, and cross over to the other side. The True Lord will carry you across. ||16||1||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਬ ਜੀਆ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਲਿ ਸਮਾਲੇ ਸੋ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਦਾਨਾ ਬੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sarab jeea pratipaal samaale so antar daanaa beenaa he |1|
lines.english: He cherishes and cares for all beings; He is deep within, the wise, all-knowing Lord. ||1||
Maaroo, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸਾ ਮੀਤੁ ਨਾਹੀ ਮੈ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: har saa meet naahee mai koee |
lines.english: I have no other friend like the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਤਨੁ ਮਨੁ ਦੀਆ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਮੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: jin tan man deea surat samoee |
lines.english: He gave me body and mind, and infused consciousness into my being.
The Guru is the sacred pool, and I am His beloved swan.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਗਰ ਮਹਿ ਰਤਨ ਲਾਲ ਬਹੁ ਸਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: saagar meh ratan laal bahu saare |
lines.english: In the ocean, there are so many jewels and rubies.
The Lord is inaccessible, inscrutable, unfathomable and unattached.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਤੀ ਮਾਣਕ ਹੀਰਾ ਹਰਿ ਜਸੁ ਗਾਵਤ ਮਨੁ ਤਨੁ ਭੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: motee maanak heeraa har jas gaavat man tan bheenaa he |2|
lines.english: The Lord's Praises are pearls, gems and diamonds. Singing His Praises, my mind and body are drenched with His Love. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਪਾਈਐ ਗੁਰ ਗੋਪਾਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: har ant na paaeeai gur gopaalaa |
lines.english: The Lord's limits cannot be found; the Guru is the Lord of the World.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਮਤਿ ਤਾਰੇ ਤਾਰਣਹਾਰਾ ਮੇਲਿ ਲਏ ਰੰਗਿ ਲੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: satigur mat taare taaranahaaraa mel le rang leenaa he |3|
lines.english: Through the Teachings of the True Guru, the Lord carries us across to the other side. He unites in His Union those who are colored by His Love. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਗਹ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਕਰੇ ਕਰਿ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਬਖਸੇ ਅਵਗੁਣ ਕੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: daragah mukat kare kar kirapaa bakhase avagun keenaa he |4|
lines.english: By His Grace, He grants liberation in His Court; He forgives them for their sins. ||4||
He has been the Friend of the Lord, from the very beginning of time, and all throughout the ages.
Without the True Guru, how can anyone be liberated?
